title: GitHub REST API - Issues Endpoint, Auth, Pagination, And Rate Limits

confirmed_facts.fact: GitHub REST repository issues endpoint is GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues. Accepts API version via X-GitHub-Api-Version header; current stable version is 2022-11-28.

Required request headers for GitHub REST API: Accept: application/vnd.github+json, Authorization: Bearer <token>, X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2022-11-28, and a non-empty User-Agent. Requests with no User-Agent are rejected.

The List repository issues endpoint supports filters: milestone, state (open|closed|all), assignee, type, creator, mentioned, labels, sort (created|updated|comments), direction (asc|desc), since, per_page (max 100), and page.
GitHub issue list endpoints may return pull requests in the response body. Pull requests can be identified and filtered by the presence of the pull_request key in each result object.

Auth options for List repository issues: fine-grained PATs (require Issues: read repository permission), GitHub App user tokens, and GitHub App installation tokens. Classic PATs use repo scope (full access) or public_repo scope (public repos only). The endpoint works unauthenticated for public repos only.
GitHub recommends fine-grained personal access tokens over classic PATs for personal REST API use. GitHub Apps are recommended when using the API on behalf of an organization or another user.
For classic PATs in organizations enforcing SAML SSO, the token must be explicitly authorized for SSO after creation. Fine-grained PATs handle SSO authorization during token creation.
GitHub paginates REST results. When paginated, the response includes a Link header with rel="next" and rel="last" URL relations. Maximum page size is 100 for the issues endpoint.

GitHub primary rate limits: 60 requests/hour unauthenticated, 5000 requests/hour authenticated. Rate-limit state exposed in response headers: x-ratelimit-limit, x-ratelimit-remaining, x-ratelimit-used, x-ratelimit-reset.
GitHub also enforces secondary rate limits (concurrency and endpoint burst protections). Honor the retry-after header when present.
GitHub security guidance requires minimum permissions, no hardcoded tokens, and secrets stored in a secure manager such as Azure Key Vault.

implementation_notes: Recommended query defaults for export-oriented pulls: state=all, sort=updated, direction=desc, per_page=100.
For repositories with more than 100 issues, loop over the page parameter until the returned page is empty.
Always filter pull requests from issue results by checking for the pull_request key unless PR data is intentionally included.
Field mapping for issue documents: number, title, state, html_url, user.login, assignees[*].login, labels[*].name, milestone.title, comments, created_at, updated_at, closed_at, body.
For raw HTTP implementations, include all four required headers: Authorization, Accept, X-GitHub-Api-Version, User-Agent.
Prefer fine-grained PATs scoped to the target repository with Issues: read for private repo access.
If the organization enforces SAML SSO and a classic PAT is used, explicitly call out SSO authorization as a required setup step.
Unauthenticated access is capped at 60 requests/hour and is not suitable for operational automations.
caveats: Issue list endpoints return pull requests mixed with issues; clients must filter if only true issues are needed.
SAML SSO organizations require explicit token authorization for classic PATs after creation.
Secondary rate limits (burst/concurrency) are not publicly documented with exact thresholds; honor retry-after headers.
Link header pagination is not available in all Power Automate connector implementations — raw HTTP fallback may be required for reliable multi-page traversal.
